name: MVP Scope Contract
description: Lock features, timeline, and success metrics in writing

steps.name: Define Core Value Proposition
steps.prompt: Let's lock in your MVP scope. First, complete this sentence: 'Users can [ACTION] so that [BENEFIT]'. This is your core value proposition.
instructions: Focus on the ONE thing this MVP must do well
Should be completable in under 10 words
If you need 'and' in the sentence, you have two products
This is your 'bar napkin pitch'

inputs.label: Value Proposition
inputs.helpText: Complete the sentence - this is your core promise

inputs.label: Bar Napkin Pitch
inputs.placeholder: Explain in 10 seconds what this does...
inputs.helpText: If you can't explain it in 10 seconds, it's too complex

steps.name: List P0 Features
steps.prompt: Now list ONLY the features required for your first paying customer. Maximum 5 features. Apply the 'would someone pay for JUST this?' test to each one.
instructions: P0 = Cannot launch without it
Each feature must map to the value proposition
If it's 'nice to have', it's not P0
5 features maximum - this is a hard cap

inputs.label: P0 Feature 1
inputs.helpText: Must be specific and testable (yes/no done)

steps.name: Validate Feature Count
steps.prompt: Let me check your feature list. Each one should pass the 'would someone pay for JUST this?' test.
instructions: Review each feature against value proposition
Remove anything that's P1 or P2 disguised as P0
Combine overlapping features if possible
Challenge: Can you launch with fewer?

inputs.label: Confirm each feature is truly P0
inputs.helpText: All features pass the 'would pay for just this' test

steps.name: Define Explicit Non-Goals
steps.prompt: Now the important part: What will you NOT build in v1? List at least 5 things you'll say 'no' to. This prevents scope creep.
instructions: Include obvious requests you'll decline
Think about what users will ask for first
Mobile app, team features, integrations often here
This is your defense against 'while we're at it'

inputs.label: Additional Non-Goals (optional)
inputs.placeholder: Any other things you're explicitly not building...

steps.name: Set Launch Day Success Metrics
steps.prompt: What does success look like on launch day? Set specific, measurable targets.
instructions: First paying customer is the most important
Be realistic but ambitious
Specific numbers, not vague goals
These metrics hold you accountable

inputs.label: First Paying Customers Target
inputs.helpText: How many paying customers on launch day?

inputs.label: Launch Day Revenue Target ($)
inputs.helpText: Total revenue from launch day sales

steps.name: Set 30-Day Success Metrics
steps.prompt: Now set your 30-day targets. This is your first month reality check.
instructions: Revenue target should be achievable
Active users = people who actually use it
Churn under 20% is healthy for early stage
Calculate customers needed: target ÷ price

inputs.label: 30-Day Max Churn Rate (%)
inputs.helpText: Percentage of users who cancel in first month

steps.name: Calculate Customer Math
steps.prompt: Let me calculate the customer math. Based on your pricing and targets, here's how many customers you need...
instructions: Customers needed = Revenue target ÷ Price
This grounds expectations in reality
If numbers seem off, reconsider pricing or targets
Better to be realistic now than disappointed later

steps.name: Confirm Scope Change Policy
steps.prompt: This is a contract. Any feature addition requires: 1) Removing an existing P0 feature, OR 2) Explicit contract renegotiation with documented reasoning. No exceptions. Do you commit?
instructions: This is a serious commitment
Scope creep kills projects
Adding features = removing features OR renegotiating
Both you and AI commit to enforcing this

inputs.label: I commit to this scope
inputs.helpText: No quick additions, no scope creep

inputs.label: AI commits to flagging scope creep

steps.name: Generate MVP Contract
steps.prompt: I'll now generate your MVP Contract and save it to the vault. This is your binding scope document.
instructions: Generate CONTRACT-{slug}.md in _vault/active/
Include all features, non-goals, and metrics
Set status to LOCKED
Add signature section with date

qualityGates.errorMessage: MVP cannot have more than 5 P0 features

qualityGates.description: At least 5 explicit things NOT being built

qualityGates.errorMessage: Success metrics must be specific numbers

transition.message: MVP scope locked! Contract saved. Now let's lock in your revenue model.

pitfalls.fix: Each feature must be testable (yes/no done)
Force yourself to list 5+ things you won't do
Treat scope creep as contract violation
